_comment: Canonical BookStack destination map for the OHRM enterprise wiki. Single source of truth for every routine in this repo. Routines MUST read this file at startup (STEP 2 repo-aware bootstrap) and use specification_shelf.id as the anchor for destination discovery. Do NOT enumerate /api/books at the top level looking for 'Specifications' -- that book does not exist; 'Specification' is a SHELF. BookStack hierarchy: Shelves â†’ Books â†’ Chapters â†’ Pages. Last verified against the live wiki: 2026-05-13.

specification_shelf.name: Specification
specification_shelf._comment: Canonical name is SINGULAR ('Specification'). Routines must accept both spellings to survive a rename. NEVER fall back to /api/books at the top level -- that lists every book in the wiki, including books outside this shelf (Calls, Demos, Defect Scopes, Training Guides, etc.) which are NOT valid spec destinations.

_specification_books_comment: TOPIC-SEED HINTS ONLY -- NOT the authoritative book list. The authoritative list of books in the Specification shelf is whatever GET /api/shelves/3 returns on the current run (the routine calls this LIVE_BOOKS in SKILL.md STEP 2). These entries exist ONLY to give context about likely destinations -- do NOT hard-code them as the only valid targets.

canonical_guideline_page.name: OHRM Wiki Contribution Guidelines
canonical_guideline_page._comment: Master style guide and contribution rules for all wiki pages. Routines should reference this page when generating new pages to ensure consistent formatting.

routine_destinations._comment: Per-routine destination registry. Each routine reads its own entry at startup. Fields page_id, page_name, book_id, book_name, chapter_id (null if page is direct under book), chapter_name, release_scope, jira_project_key, epic_filter (null if routine covers full project), last_verified (ISO-8601 UTC of last manual verification), last_synced_at (ISO-8601 UTC of last successful sync run).

roster_daily_sync._history: Corrected via roster_daily_sync auto-detection on 2026-06-24T214225Z: page_id 29->548, book_id 33->36, jira_project_key RST->ROS, release_scope 8.0.2->8.1.

orange_sign_daily_sync._history: Created via FIRST-FIRE BOOTSTRAP on 2026-05-19T09:00:04Z (routine fire trig_01V9qhBPmLH6HaBrpp7pP8Nz). New Orange Sign book (id=37) + Orange Sign Core page (id=549) created in Specification shelf 3. Bootstrap committed sentinel update via feature branch claude/wizardly-newton-2E8yB; cherry-picked to main 2026-05-20.
orange_sign_daily_sync._epic_filter: RV is the Rivendell sprint-team project; it hosts many parallel features. This routine MUST scope every JQL by parent = RV-682 OR "epic link" = RV-682 (Orange Sign / In-House Esignature epic). Without the filter the routine would pull every Rivendell-team story at fixVersion 8.0.2, contaminating the spec page with unrelated content.

exclusions._comment: Page IDs and book IDs that routines must never overwrite. Typically system pages, index pages, and the canonical guideline page.
exclusions.reason: Pages 1-3 are system bootstrap pages created during initial wiki setup. Page 1 is the canonical guideline page. Do not overwrite.
exclusions._versioning_note: OrangeHRM Wiki uses BookStack. BookStack page revisions are managed server-side. The wiki_destination.json does not track page revision history -- that is stored in BookStack's revision log accessible via GET /api/pages/{id}/export/html or the BookStack UI.
exclusions._deprecation_note: When a book or page is deprecated (removed from the live wiki), add its ID to the appropriate protected list and set deprecated_at on the nav_tree entry. Routines will skip deprecated destinations and log a warning.
exclusions._scope_note: release_scope values like '8.0.2' refer to Jira fixVersion strings, not BookStack versions. The wiki does not use version numbers -- it is a living document updated continuously.
exclusions._multi_epic_note: Some routines cover multiple epics (e.g., cs_features_daily_sync may cover several CS epics). In those cases, epic_filter will be a comma-separated string or an array. Currently all active routines use a single epic filter or null.
exclusions._book_creation_note: New books are created by routines during FIRST-FIRE BOOTSTRAP when the target book does not yet exist in the shelf. The routine creates the book via POST /api/books, then creates the target page, then commits the new IDs back to wiki_destination.json. See SKILL.md Â§15 for the full bootstrap protocol.
exclusions._nav_tree_note: The specification_nav_tree is updated by any routine that performs a live shelf walk (STEP 2.6). The tree is a snapshot, not a live view. Routines must always call GET /api/shelves/3 to get the live tree before making structural decisions.

_field_formats.last_synced_at: ISO-8601 compact UTC: YYYY-MM-DDTHHMMSNZ (no colons in time, e.g. 2026-05-20T091537Z)
_field_formats.last_synced_commit_sha: Full 40-char GitHub commit SHA of the commit that wrote this nav-tree update
_field_formats.last_verified: ISO-8601 full UTC with colons: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (manual verification timestamp)
_field_formats.deprecated_at: ISO-8601 full UTC with colons: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (set when page/book removed from live wiki)
